km.Wales, United Kingdom: វេលស៍, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf是Leuaf ap Nynnid的兒子，於1235年出生在英國威爾斯。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf是Leuaf ap Nynnid的儿子，于1235年出生在英国威尔斯。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Leuaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English lef, leff, lief, life ‘beloved’ (Old English lēof ‘beloved; dear one, sweetheart’).

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English personal name Lef. This could represent either Old Norse Leifr (see Leif 1) or Old English Lēof which, though unrecorded, is a very probable short form of Old English names such as Lēofrīc, Lēofwine. Compare Leif 3.
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv: ornamental name from löv ‘leaf’. Compare Leif 2.

variants.name: Leif
meanings: Swedish: from the Old Norse personal name Leifr, a short form of the compound names with the second element leifr (from leif ‘heir’).
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv (see Leaf 3).
English: rare variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Levick
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Lefeke, Old English Lēofeca, a derivative of Lēofa (see Leaf ).
English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French l'eveske ‘the bishop’ (see Bishop ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Yiddish Leyvik, a pet form of the personal name Leyvi, itself a form of the Biblical name Levi (see Levy ).

variants.name: Leacock
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Levecok, a pet form of Leve, see Leaf .
variant of   Laycock  .

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Life
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Liff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yechiel, Chaim.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish: variant of   Lief  .

variants.name: Lief
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Varda, Meyer.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Livonia (now a part of Latvia), from German Liv.

variants.name: Leef
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

nameMeanings.name: Awr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: West African (Nigeria): Yoruba name of unexplained etymology.
German: variant of   Aue  .

variants.name: Aue
meanings: From Old Norse Ari, originally a byname meaning ‘eagle’, or else a short form of any of the compound names with this first element.

en.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf was born in 1235, in Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Leuaf ap Nynnid. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf est né en 1235, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, fils de Leuaf ap Nynnid. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf wurde 1235 in Wales, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Leuaf ap Nynnid geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf è nato il nel 1235 nel Galles, Regno Unito come figlio di Leuaf ap Nynnid. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuafは1235年にイギリスウェールズでLeuaf ap Nynnidの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf(은)는 1235년 영국 웨일스에서 Leuaf ap Nynnid의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf nasceu em 1235, em Gales, United Kingdom filho de Leuaf ap Nynnid. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf родился в 1235 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. Его отца звали Leuaf ap Nynnid. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Awr ab Leuaf nació en 1235, en Gales, Reino Unido como hijo de Leuaf ap Nynnid. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
